title: Getting Started with EasyStat
text: EasyStat implements four-step pipeline transforms raw data publication-ready statistical output single function call: Core Statistical Engine — wraps base-R stats functions (lm, t.test, aov, etc.) Metric Extractor — uses broom::tidy() / broom::glance() extract key values (p-value, effect size, CIs, df) Narrative Generator Module — applies conditional logic produce statistically sound, plain-language interpretation Unified Result Object — returns easystat_result S3 object prints HTML RStudio Viewer ASCII console, can exported Word

title: Getting Started with EasyStat
text: Every EasyStat function returns list class "easystat_result": can access field directly:
code: result <- easy_ttest(mpg ~ am, data = mtcars) cat(result$explanation) #> INDEPENDENT-SAMPLES t-TEST #> Comparison: mpg ~ am #>  #> An independent-samples t-test revealed a statistically significant (p = 0.1374%) difference between the two groups (t(18.33) = -3.767). The mean for '0' was 17.1474 and the mean for '1' was 24.3923. The 95% confidence interval for the difference in means ranged from -11.2802 to -3.2097. These results provide statistically significant evidence that '0' and '1' differ meaningfully on the measured variable.

title: EasyStat — Automated Statistical Analysis & Narrative Reporting
text: EasyStat bridges gap statistical output actionable insight. single function call delivers statistical result, plain-language narrative interpretation, publication-ready tables, rendered RStudio Viewer, R console, Microsoft Word. User-facing p-values reported percentages rounded 4 decimal places, raw model objects still retain original numeric p-values advanced use.

title: EasyStat — Automated Statistical Analysis & Narrative Reporting
code: # From CRAN (when available) install.packages("EasyStat")  # Development version from GitHub # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("itsmdivakaran/EasyStat")  # From local source install.packages("path/to/EasyStat", repos = NULL, type = "source")

title: EasyStat — Automated Statistical Analysis & Narrative Reporting
code: library(EasyStat)  # Linear regression with narrative easy_regression(mpg ~ wt + hp, data = mtcars)  # Logistic regression with odds ratios easy_logistic_regression(am ~ mpg + wt, data = mtcars)  # t-test easy_ttest(mpg ~ am, data = mtcars)  # One-way ANOVA easy_anova(Sepal.Length ~ Species, data = iris)  # Descriptive statistics for multiple variables easy_describe(mtcars, vars = c("mpg", "hp", "wt"))  # Correlation heatmap easy_correlation_heatmap(mtcars, vars = c("mpg", "hp", "wt", "qsec", "drat"))  # Export any result to Word result <- easy_logistic_regression(am ~ mpg + wt, data = mtcars) export_to_word(result, file = "report.docx", title = "Transmission Model",                author = "Mahesh Divakaran, Gunjan Singh, Jayadevan Shreedharan")

title: Run a One-Way ANOVA with Automated Narrative Reporting — easy_anova
text: Executes one-way ANOVA using stats::aov(), extracts key metrics via broom, computes eta-squared effect-size measure, generates plain-language narrative via Narrative Generator Module.

title: Run a One-Way ANOVA with Automated Narrative Reporting — easy_anova
code: easy_anova(formula, data, alpha = 0.05)

title: Run a One-Way ANOVA with Automated Narrative Reporting — easy_anova
text: formula formula form outcome ~ group_factor, character string. Passed directly stats::aov(). data data frame containing variables formula. alpha Significance threshold narrative. Default 0.05.

title: Run a One-Way ANOVA with Automated Narrative Reporting — easy_anova
text: object class "easystat_result" : test_type Character: "anova" formula_str Character string formula used raw_model raw aov object coefficients_table ANOVA table (SS, df, MS, F, p) model_fit_table Summary metrics (F-statistic, eta-squared, p-value) explanation Plain-language narrative string

title: EasyStat 2.0.0
text: Added easy_logistic_regression() binary logistic regression odds ratios, approximate odds-ratio confidence intervals, likelihood-ratio model tests, McFadden pseudo-R2, automated narrative reporting. Added richer supporting tables: ANOVA now includes group descriptives, assumption checks, Tukey post-hoc comparisons. Chi-square tests now include observed contingency tables, expected counts, row percentages, column percentages, total percentages. Regression now includes ANOVA, diagnostic, influential-observation tables. Added easy_wilcox() easy_kruskal() common non-parametric workflows. Added regression figures: easy_regression_diagnostics() easy_odds_ratio_plot(). Added comprehensive descriptive statistics: easy_describe() easy_group_summary() 21 statistics per variable (mean, median, mode, SD, variance, skewness, kurtosis, normality test, CI, ). Extended inferential test suite four new functions: easy_chisq(): Chi-square test independence goodness--fit, Cramer’s V effect size. easy_ztest(): One- two-sample z-tests Cohen’s d. easy_ftest(): Levene-style F-test equality variances via stats::var.test(), variance ratio CI. easy_correlation(): Pearson/Spearman/Kendall bivariate pairwise matrix correlation confidence intervals. New visualization functions (return easystat_result objects plain-language interpretations): easy_histogram(): distribution histogram normal-curve overlay. easy_boxplot(): grouped box--whisker plots outlier marking. easy_scatter(): scatter plot regression line R-squared label. easy_barplot(): count mean (+/- SE) bar charts group support. easy_qqplot(): Q-Q normality plot. easy_density(): kernel density curve, optionally grouped. easy_correlation_heatmap(): annotated pairwise correlation heatmap. easy_autoplot(): smart dispatcher picks right plot type given easystat_result. New theme: theme_easystat() — professional ggplot2 theme color system applied uniformly across visualization functions. Word export: export_to_word() creates fully formatted .docx report (via flextable officer) title, authors, narrative, tables.

title: EasyStat 2.0.0
text: User-facing p-values now display percentages rounded 4 decimal places across result tables, plot summaries, narrative text. Narrative Generator Module expanded 15 conditional narrative templates covering new test plot types. functions return unified easystat_result S3 object consistent fields: test_type, formula_str, raw_model, coefficients_table, model_fit_table, explanation. HTML Viewer output (print.easystat_result) redesigned improved typography responsive layout.
